meaning: adj.鐧句竾鍊嶇殑adv.鐧句竾鍊嶅湴
examples: The stem cells themselves are extracted from cattle muscle and then multiplied a millionfold before they are put in Petri dishes and allowed to turn into muscle cells.
By 2005 the world's bandwidth could grow a millionfold, making it "virtually free and virtually infinite, " says James Rothnie, an EMCsenior vice president.
During this period, computational power has advanced more than a millionfold.
The demand rises so fast that some big data haulers increase their capacity tenfold every year--a millionfold increase in six years, with no end in sight.
